titleStmt.title: Letter from Peter Mooney to his sister Katie Mooney, 3 November 1915
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Peter Mooney

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Peter Mooney (b.1886) to his sister, Katie (b.1884). Peter thanks Katie for the pouch of tobacco that was sent his way. He reports that the war seems to be getting no better, possibly referring to the British failure to break the German lines at Loos. Peter requests a nail scissors and the addresses of family and friends at home to write to. He closes with reference to the increasing number of recruits arriving to the front, wondering where they must be coming from. The recruits likely came from the British New Army regiments (so called Kitchener's Army), civilian volunteers who made up the depleted ranks of the British forces in 1915.Orphaned at age 7, Peter and his sister Katie were raised by their uncle William and his wife Mary in Caulstown. While there are no surviving enlistment papers for Peter, his War Badge Index Record shows his enlistment date as 18 October 1915. He served with the Royal Artillery and later transferred to the Royal Engineers where he was promoted to the rank of Sergeant. He served in several places at the Western Front, including Ypres, St. Omer and Passchendael. Peter and Kate were avid correspondents. This letter part of a collection of over ninety surviving letters.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Nov 3rd1915 Dear Kate, I received your letter
this morning alright. I was
glad to receive the papers
The war seems to be getting
no better. I forgot to tell
you that I got the tobacco
alright I will write when
I want more. When you
are writing again try & enclose
Christy Mulans address as
I would like to write to him
Tell Pat that I was asking
for him. You might send me
on a very small sciziors for
cutting my nails. Recruits
are coming in here every day
the wonder is where they are
coming from. You will have
to forgive me for writing such
a short letter. I will write
on Sunday again. I will now
say GoodâBye to all for
the present. Your fond Brother Peter Mooney
Miss. K. Mooney Caulstown Dunboyne Co. Meath
